title: Missing reentrancy guards on critical entry points
reason: Generic missing-guard observation without a concrete reentrant path to protocol-level fund loss; ERC20 calls shown do not by themselves demonstrate exploitable reentrancy here.

title: Cross-chain liquidation validation can be front-run on destination chain
reason: This is normal liquidation competition behavior, not a protocol integrity bug; no unique exploit against protocol funds/state was shown.

title: No minimum liquidation amount check enables dust attacks
reason: No realistic protocol-level harm beyond optional gas inefficiency; dust liquidations are generally permissible design choices.

title: Repay functions trust caller approval without verification
reason: `safeTransferFrom` already enforces allowance/balance by reverting atomically; claimed partial-state inconsistency is not supported.

title: borrowForCrossChain transfers tokens before recording borrow position
reason: In this flow, later failures revert the full transaction (including the subcall token transfer), so no persistent no-debt payout window was validated.

title: getHypotheticalAccountLiquidityCollateral may double-count same-chain and cross-chain borrows
reason: Summing same-chain and cross-chain debts is expected when both exist; candidate did not show actual same-debt duplication in storage semantics.

title: Cross-chain collateral update loses precision on index refresh
reason: Integer truncation dust from standard fixed-point division is minimal and not shown to create material exploitability beyond negligible rounding.

title: borrowCrossChain allows zero-value LayerZero messages due to check order
reason: Claim is not supported by code path; borrow amount must be non-zero and message fee/gas handling is not tied to borrow amount in the alleged way.

title: withdrawEth lacks event logging for accounting transparency
reason: Operational observability issue only; not a reportable security vulnerability under protocol-harm criteria.
